statement_a: Chapter 2 regimental passage calls Mehdi Khan a son of Naser al-Molk.
statement_b: Chapter 4 states that Naser al-Molk had sons Hossein-Ali and Mohsen and one daughter.
notes: Do not merge Mehdi Khan into the confirmed child list without further evidence.

statement_b: Later narrative and documentary caption use Ehtesham al-Dowleh.
notes: Both forms remain attached to P0024; chronology of formal title change remains open.

statement_a: Caption identifies Hossein Khan Hesam al-Molk I, father of Amir Afkham.
statement_b: Canonical narrative person is Mohammad-Hossein Khan Hesam al-Molk P0022.
notes: Probable identity, but exact name form must be verified from a better scan or another source.

statement_a: Figure 4 places Haji Mina Khan among the five sons of Haji Abdollah Khan.
statement_b: The Chapter 2 prose can be read as naming Haji Mina Khan among the sons of Haji Mohammad Jafar Khan.
notes: The canonical graph currently follows Figure 4 with a disputed edge. Do not upgrade this parentage until the prose/diagram discrepancy is resolved.

conflict_topic: immediate children of Haji Fazlollah Khan
statement_a: The user-supplied reconstruction of Figure 4 shows four immediate children: Mohammad Hassan Khan, Ali Khan, Hossein Ali Khan, and Abdolhossein Khan Farkhari.
statement_b: The family-history summary in Majmooe Asaar appears to name three sons and uses a different formulation, including Haji Mohammad Hossein.
notes: The canonical graph follows the archive owner’s row-by-row Figure 4 reconstruction. The comparison-source discrepancy remains open and must not be silently merged.
